contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 60
- Description: Credential vault V1 — laptop disaster-recovery, encrypted iCloud snapshots via launchd
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/248
  
  Details:
  Shipped V1 of the credential vault Gary asked for: 'if I fuck up my MacBook I can restore the entire context/everything easily.' Scope = ~46 credential files across ~/Applications/*/{.env,config/,credentials/}, ~/.clasprc-*.json, ~/.config/gh/, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.aws/ — everything not in any git repo.
  
  Architecture:
  - Explicit allowlist manifest (CREDENTIAL_MANIFEST.txt) — no globs, auditable.
  - backup_credentials.sh tars manifest paths and pipes through openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -pbkdf2 -iter 600000 with a 0600 passphrase file (LastPass-managed).
  - Output lands in ~/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/credential_vault/ with 30-snapshot retention + credentials-latest.age symlink.
  - restore_credentials.sh is the inverse: defaults to latest snapshot, --keep-old-files (skip-on-conflict) by default, supports --dry-run / --list / --force.
  - ~/Library/LaunchAgents/me.truesight.credential-backup.plist triggers backup via WatchPaths on credential parent dirs + 03:00 nightly heartbeat. ThrottleInterval 120s + script-level 60s debounce.
  
  Pivoted from age to openssl enc because age has no passphrase-from-file flag (kills launchd unattended use). LibreSSL 3.3.6 on macOS supports -pbkdf2 -iter natively, so restore needs no brew install.
  
  Smoke-tested end-to-end: 26 paths encrypted → 245 KB snapshot → decrypted to scratch HOME → diff -q confirmed byte-identical roundtrip on .clasprc-admin.json, dao_client/.env, and id_rsa.
  
  Threat model + first-time setup + disaster-recovery runbook + maintenance procedures captured in CREDENTIAL_VAULT.md. Cross-linked from GOOGLE_API_CREDENTIALS.md (the prior 'open follow-up' stub) and added a memory pointer.
  
  What's NOT shipped (intentional):
  - launchd plist lives on Gary's laptop only.
  - Passphrase file lives only on the laptop (Gary needs to plant it from LastPass before first real backup).
  - Memory dir vaulting (~/.claude/projects/*/memory/) — flagged in doc 'Open follow-ups'.
  - Host-side credential provisioning for EC2 fleet (different problem, tracked in OPEN_FOLLOWUPS.md AWS Secrets Manager entry).
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 100.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
